research.title: Deep Explore: Skills Storage Refactoring — JSON-blob to File-Based Directories
research.topic: Jak przebudować warstwę storage skills z monolitycznego JSON na plikowy model z git sync
research.summary: Analiza opcji storage dla skills. Rekomendacja: katalogowy model (_global/skills/{name}/) z _config.json + SKILL.md + pliki, osobny git clone forge-skills.git, source-of-truth split między frontmatter i _config.json.
key_findings: Obecny skills.json trzyma cały markdown + pliki jako stringi — nie skaluje się i nie wspiera git diffs
Katalogowy model: _config.json (Forge metadata) + SKILL.md (portable content) + real files
Git sync: osobny clone, Forge zarządza pull/push, flaga sync per skill
Category model wymaga zmiany na wielokrotny (categories: string[])
Nowy SkillStorageService + GitSyncService w backendzie
Frontend: drag&drop move, folder/zip upload, git sync UI

research.title: Test Content Write
key_findings: content field works

research.title: Deep-Explore: Debug Console v2 Options
research.topic: How to fix and improve the debug console (BottomPanel, LLM Monitor, Event Stream, API Inspector, AI Sidebar Debug)
research.summary: Six problem areas mapped with 3+ options each. Recommended: prop-based slug passing for LLM Monitor/EventStream, reversed render order for API Inspector, new Debug tab in AI Sidebar with structured stream parser, ErrorContext component for enriched errors, CSS user-select fix + request detail panel, and consistent design tokens with JSON syntax highlighting.
key_findings: LLM Monitor and Event Stream break because useParams() returns undefined outside ProjectLayout - fix is passing slug as prop from global project store or URL parsing
API Inspector renders oldest-first because debugStore appends to tail and component renders in array order - simplest fix is .toReversed() in render
AI Sidebar streaming already works via WS chat.token events - structured Debug tab needs a parser that groups tokens by content_block type (thinking, tool_use, text)
Error messages are generic because chatStore catches errors and only extracts .message - enriching at api.ts interceptor level gives all components access to status, endpoint, body excerpt
Response bodies likely use CSS that prevents text selection - fix is user-select: text on pre/code elements + adding request params panel
Visual inconsistency across debug components - a shared DebugTheme with design tokens (colors, spacing, font sizes) + JSON highlighter would unify the look

research.title: Deep-Risk: Debug Console v2 Risk Assessment
research.topic: What can go wrong when refactoring debug console components and adding new features
research.summary: 7 risks identified, top 3: infinite re-render loops from new Debug tab subscriptions (composite 16), WS event format changes breaking structured parser (composite 15), and scope creep from large appetite turning medium task into full rewrite (composite 14). All mitigatable with incremental delivery and defensive coding patterns.
key_findings: React infinite re-render risk is HIGH - lesson L-018 documents exact same pattern (context setState in useEffect). Debug tab subscribing to WS events in useEffect must use ref+subscription, not state updates per token
WS chat.token event format may not include content_block type metadata needed for structured view - if backend only sends raw text deltas, parser cannot distinguish thinking vs response
Large appetite + 6 KRs risks scope creep - incremental delivery (fix bugs first, then add features, then redesign) mitigates this
debugStore circular buffer at 200 entries may cause memory issues if Debug tab creates additional subscriptions - monitor with React DevTools profiler
Reversing API Inspector order could break existing user muscle memory - but current behavior is clearly wrong (newest should be first)
EventStream max 50 events is too low for active projects - increasing to 200 aligns with debugStore but increases memory footprint
JSON syntax highlighting library choice affects bundle size - react-json-view is 150KB+ gzipped, custom highlighter is lighter but more work

research.title: Deep-Architect: Debug Console v2 Architecture
research.topic: Component architecture for refactored debug console with structured stream view
research.summary: Architecture centers on 3 ADRs: (1) prop-based slug with URL extraction over useParams, (2) ref+subscription pattern for Debug tab stream over React state, (3) shared DebugTheme design tokens over ad-hoc styling. Component decomposition: BottomPanel shell -> 3 tab panels (ApiInspector, LlmMonitor, EventStream) receiving slug as prop, AISidebar gets 5th Debug tab with StreamView component using useRef for token accumulation.
key_findings: BottomPanel needs slug extraction from window.location.pathname (not useParams) - regex /projects/([^/]+)/ - passed as prop to children
New StreamView component: useRef for token buffer, content_block parser groups into ThinkingBlock/ToolCallBlock/ResponseBlock, 100ms throttled React state updates
ApiError class extends Error with status/url/method/responseExcerpt - thrown by api.ts, caught and rendered by ErrorDetail component
RequestDetailPanel: collapsible sections (Headers, Query Params, Request Body, Response Body, Timing) with JsonView for syntax highlighting
DebugTheme: CSS custom properties (--debug-bg, --debug-border, --debug-text-*, --debug-status-*) applied via data-debug-panel attribute
Debug tab in AISidebar subscribes to chatStore streaming events via new useStreamDebug() hook - separate from chat rendering
